channel.title: Hydrology on Aviral Agarwal
channel.description: Recent content in Hydrology on Aviral Agarwal

item.title: Soil Moisture Balance Model
item.description:
Overview

Developed an R-based soil moisture balance model to simulate daily crop water use and accurately estimate irrigation demand across a 100,000+ acre agricultural basin in California. The model integrated climate, crop, soil, and land use data to dynamically track soil moisture and drive water demand calculations.

Groundwater and MODFLOW

To evaluate groundwater sustainability, I executed MODFLOW 6 simulations using FloPy, modeling various pumping scenarios to assess spatial and temporal stress on the aquifer system. This analysis supported decision-making for one of California’s most over-drafted groundwater basins, providing key insights for sustainable water management strategies under SGMA, including conjunctive use, managed aquifer recharge, and demand reduction planning.

item.title: Water Resiliency Model for Central Coast California
item.description:
Overview

Analyzed climate change impacts on Central California water infrastructure using GIS and DWR data to assess basin vulnerabilities. Provided data-driven insights that supported five regional water management plans.

Resilience index

Led a team of three in developing a Python script computing a dynamic resilience index for San Luis Obispo County, integrating 10+ data sources on water demand, supply, and climate projections to enhance drought preparedness efforts.

item.title: Frequency Analysis of Non-Stationary Hydrological Extremes in Northern Ohio
item.description:
Overview

Performed frequency analysis on stream flows and precipitation in northern Ohio using the Vogel et al. (2011) method, utilizing 76 years of data from 133 stream gauges.

Findings

Assessed the impact of trends on extreme events over the next 20 years for 100- to 500-year return periods through the application of magnification factors and reduced return periods.

item.title: Flood Mitigation Analysis for Foster City
item.description:
Overview

Executed a data-driven flood analysis using concepts of expected loss and exceedance probability along with 10,000 Monte Carlo simulations.

Outcomes

Proposed a hybrid solution combining insurance and levees, reducing potential annual losses by 40% and eliminating risk of $400K+ damages.
